rationale: Villanueva et al. (2008) is cited in a methodological argument, not just as general background. The authors explicitly state they build on earlier models with asymmetric lag lengths and then claim their DASVAR model goes beyond those models. This citation helps define the paper's incremental contribution in model development, making the role substantively tied to framing and method positioning.
relation_description: The citing paper positions its DASVAR approach as building on prior marketing VAR/SVAR work exemplified by Villanueva et al. (2008), especially the use of directional structural effects and different lag lengths across equations. It extends that line by adding loss-versus-gain asymmetry and history-dependent impulse responses for service-crisis effects on perceived quality.
